firstBlock.name: NORTH LONDON COLLEGIATE SCHOOL SINGAPORE
firstBlock.body:
21. NORTH LONDON COLLEGIATE SCHOOL SINGAPORE -- ~SGD 55,733/year (senior years) -- British/IB -- Depot Road, Queenstown

Data tier: FULL BRIEFING

THE VIBE

NLCS Singapore opened in September 2020, added a Sixth Form in August 2021, and graduated its first IB cohort in May 2023 -- so it's still a young school by Singapore standards, even if the UK brand stretches back to 1850. The campus is purpose-built and genuinely impressive on paper: a 700-seat performing arts centre, a 50-metre pool, a gym, roof gardens, and an outdoor plaza, all packed onto a constrained site on Depot Road in Queenstown. WhichSchoolAdvisor notes it feels "slightly cramped" given its proximity to a busy road and no room to expand -- important context for a school charging these fees.

The curriculum runs from Pre-KG through Grade 12, with a British National Curriculum-style approach in the junior years leading to the IB Diploma (no IGCSE pathway). WhichSchoolAdvisor describes it as "academically rigorous" and "academically selective," which the parent survey data bears out: survey respondents are satisfied with academic outcomes but rate sport and the arts below the peer average.

IB results so far: Class of 2023 averaged 38.77 with 47% scoring 40+; Class of 2024 averaged 36.52; Class of 2025 averaged 36.7 with a top score of 45 and 100% pass rate. These are school-reported figures. The 2023 number, in particular, is strong for a debut cohort -- though some drop in the 2024/25 average is worth noting, and cohort sizes remain small enough that averages can shift significantly year to year.

One notable data point: the Financial Times published an investigation (January 2026, paywalled) titled "Elite British private school scraps Singapore expansion plans," referencing allegations of safeguarding lapses and workplace bullying and misogyny. NLCS Singapore has also reportedly walked back expansion plans.

THE TRADE-OFFS

The teacher forum picture is one of the more troubling in the Singapore expat school scene. Multiple threads on r/Internationalteachers contain detailed, consistent allegations: toxic leadership, bullying within the workplace, high staff turnover, Saturday working expectations, poor resource provision, building defects including mould and flooding, and pay described as "around 20% less than Tanglin Trust School" with a poor rent allowance. One commenter wrote: "None stayed more than two years. None had good things to say about it. None recommended it. All warned against considering it as an option. Universal damnation is rare..." Another commenter said: "The school is owned by a for profit company who limit resources and cut corners."

This matters for parents not because teachers complaining online is unusual, but because high staff turnover directly affects continuity of teaching -- exactly the thing parents paying premium fees are paying for.

On the regulatory side: Singapore's PDPC fined NLCS Singapore S$10,000 in 2022 for failing to put reasonable security arrangements in place after unauthorised access to student applicants' personal data.

Pros:
- IB Diploma averages in the mid-to-high 30s from the first three cohorts, with strong 40+ rates
- Purpose-built modern campus with a 700-seat performing arts centre and 50-metre pool
- Clear academic identity -- unambiguously IB-focused
- 100% parent recommendation rate in WhichSchoolAdvisor's survey
- University offers to Oxbridge, Imperial, US Ivies, and leading Canadian universities
- Central Queenstown/Buona Vista location

Cons:
- Multiple consistent allegations of poor staff culture, high turnover, and heavy workload on teacher forums
- Financial Times investigation (Jan 2026) reportedly raises safeguarding and workplace misconduct concerns
- Expansion plans reportedly scrapped
- Campus is "slightly cramped" with no room to grow
- No IGCSE pathway -- straight to IB DP
- Among the most expensive schools in Singapore
- IB cohort averages dropped from 38.77 (2023) to 36.52 (2024)
- PDPC fine for data protection failure (2022)

Data confidence: Moderate.

Questions to ask on an open day:
- What is your current staff turnover rate, and how has it changed since the school opened?
- The Financial Times published a piece in January 2026 referencing safeguarding and workplace culture concerns -- what can you tell us about that?
- Why have the expansion plans been scaled back?
- What support exists for students who find the IB Diploma stretch too far?
- Can you share the full IB cohort results for the last three years, including the distribution of scores?

---
